# Sugi Atlas

A comprehensive bio atlas of human genes (drugs and diseases in development).
Every fact on every page traces deterministically through the biobtree
identifier graph back to its primary source (NCBI, UniProt, EBI, etc.).
The executive summary at the top of each page is LLM-generated and clearly
disclosed; all data tables and identifiers below it are built by code, not
by an LLM.

## Pages

- [Atlas — entry](/atlas/)
- [Genes catalog](/atlas/gene/)
- [Drugs catalog](/atlas/drug/) — in development
- [Diseases catalog](/atlas/disease/) — in development
- [Example gene page: TP53](/atlas/gene/TP53/)

## Machine-readable artifacts alongside every entity page

For each `/atlas/<type>/<id>/` there is:

- `entity.jsonld` — schema.org Gene (or Drug / MedicalCondition) with
  `sameAs` links to authority sources (NCBI, UniProt, Ensembl, HGNC, OMIM,
  ClinVar, Reactome, etc.). Use this as the federated-identity anchor.
- `provenance.json` — schema.org Dataset. For every section of the page it
  lists the datasets touched, the biobtree chains used, and the upstream
  primary sources with URLs. Use this when you need to cite a specific fact
  with its source.
- `bundle.json` — raw structured data (the section bundles the page was
  rendered from). Convenient for programmatic ingest.
- `body.md` / `summary.md` / `judge.json` / `body_gate.json` — audit
  sidecars; not needed for normal citation, useful for reproducibility.

## How to cite a specific fact

1. Fetch `/atlas/gene/<SYMBOL>/entity.jsonld` (or use the same-URL HTML page
   and read the inline `<script type="application/ld+json">` block).
2. Use the page anchors (every `##` section header is an HTML id) to point
   at the section that holds the fact: e.g.
   `/atlas/gene/TP53/#disease-associations`.
3. To trace the fact back to its primary source, fetch
   `/atlas/gene/<SYMBOL>/provenance.json` and look at the section's
   `upstream_sources` list.

## Disclosure

- The executive summary block (under `## Summary` on each page) is written
  by an LLM and labeled accordingly. It restates facts already present in
  the page body; the body itself is deterministic.
- Atlas does not invent identifiers or counts.

## Project + repo

- Codebase: https://github.com/tamerh/sugi-atlas (open source).
- Built on biobtree: https://biobtree.org.
- Hugo static site (no JS required to read the data).

## Contact

- Issues / corrections: https://github.com/tamerh/sugi-atlas/issues
- Site: https://sugi.bio
